skill.name: concise-planning
skill.description: Generate concise, actionable coding plans with clear scope, ordered tasks, validation steps, and limited open questions.
skill.summary: Creates atomic coding task plans with scope, action items, validation, and concise questions.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static external command findings are false positives from Markdown inline code and a fenced Markdown plan template. The weak cryptography finding at SKILL.md:3 is also a false positive, with no cryptographic algorithm or malicious intent present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Code
low_findings.description: SKILL.md:16 uses Markdown inline code to reference README.md and related files. It does not execute a shell command or invoke an external process.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is plain Markdown documentation. There is no executable script, shell syntax, or user input execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Plan Template Fence
low_findings.description: SKILL.md:35 starts a fenced Markdown example for the expected plan format. The block is documentation content, not Ruby backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The fenced block is labeled markdown and contains checklist placeholders. It is not executable code and does not run commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: SKILL.md:3 contains the skill description only. No evidence found of weak cryptographic algorithms, hashing, encryption, or security-sensitive code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is frontmatter prose describing the planning skill. It contains no cryptographic operation or library reference.

content.user_title: Plan Coding Work with Atomic Checklists
content.value_statement: Coding requests can become vague or too broad before implementation starts. This skill turns them into concise plans with scope, ordered tasks, validation, and focused questions.
actual_capabilities: Scans relevant project context before planning when files are available.
Separates planned work into in-scope and out-of-scope items.
Creates ordered action items that are concrete and verb-first.
Includes at least one validation or testing step in each plan.
Limits clarification questions to blocking issues.
limitations: It produces plans only and does not implement code by itself.
Plan quality depends on available repository context and user detail.
It does not verify technical feasibility unless supporting files are provided.
It is optimized for coding tasks, not general project management.

use_cases.title: Prepare Feature Work
use_cases.description: Convert a feature request into a scoped checklist before editing code.

use_cases.title: Review Implementation Scope
use_cases.description: Clarify what belongs in a change and what should wait for later work.

use_cases.title: Create Onboarding Tasks
use_cases.description: Break a small repository task into steps a new contributor can follow.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Coding Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need a simple plan for a small code change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a concise plan for this coding task: [describe task]. Include scope, action items, validation, and any blocking questions.

prompt_templates.title: Plan from Repository Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the assistant can inspect files before planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the relevant project files, then create an atomic implementation plan for: [describe task]. Keep questions limited to blockers.

prompt_templates.title: Scoped Refactor Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a refactor needs boundaries and validation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a refactor for [module or behavior]. Separate in-scope and out-of-scope work, then list ordered steps and test coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Delivery Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when planning a larger change with rollout concerns.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a delivery plan for [change]. Include discovery, implementation, validation, rollout, and no more than three open questions.

output_examples.input: Plan how to add password reset emails to an existing web app.
output_examples.output: A short plan with context review, scoped email-token work, implementation steps, tests for token expiry, and rollout notes.

output_examples.input: Plan a cleanup of duplicated validation logic across two services.
output_examples.output: A scoped refactor checklist that identifies shared logic, moves behavior carefully, updates callers, and validates both services.

output_examples.input: Plan how to fix a flaky checkout test.
output_examples.output: A focused debugging plan with reproduction steps, likely timing checks, a targeted fix step, and repeated validation.

best_practices: Provide the task goal and any known constraints before asking for a plan.
Share relevant file names or modules when repository access is limited.
Use the generated checklist as a living plan during implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a substitute for code review or testing.
Do not ask for broad product roadmaps without a concrete coding task.
Do not accept assumptions silently when a plan lists blocking questions.

faq.question: Does this skill write code?
faq.answer: No. It creates implementation plans and checklists for coding tasks.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: It supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it inspect a repository?
faq.answer: Yes, when the active tool can read project files provided in the workspace.

faq.question: How detailed are the plans?
faq.answer: Plans are concise and usually include scope, six to ten action items, validation, and open questions.

faq.question: Is it useful for non-coding work?
faq.answer: It is designed for coding tasks. General planning may need a different skill.

faq.question: Does it ask many questions first?
faq.answer: No. It asks only one or two questions when missing information blocks a useful plan.
